title: Lead AI Software Engineer- AI Enablement
description:
Our Purpose  Mastercard powers economies and empowers people
in 200+ countries and territories worldwide. Together with
our customers, we're helping build a sustainable economy
where everyone can prosper. We support a wide range of
digital payments choices, making transactions secure,
simple, smart and accessible. Our technology and innovation,
partnerships and networks combine to deliver a unique set of
products and services that help people, businesses and
governments realize their greatest potential. Title and
Summary Lead AI Software Engineer- AI Enablement Overview:
The Mastercard Cloud Team, in Mastercard's ONE division,
owns and drives cloud usage and adoption for the company.
The team has a unique opportunity to drive cloud patterns,
standards, and best practices across all of Mastercard and
their M&A's. The cloud team has a unique opportunity to be
at the forefront of an evolving landscape of technology at
Mastercard and help shape the future of development,
deployment and more! This cloud engineering role is focused
on building a holistic platform for Generative AI Large
Language Models (LLMs). The Role:• Design, configure, and
implement a Gen. AI platform, including MLOps/LLMOps for
Gen. AI LLMs, both commercial and open source• Ensure
alignment to appropriate patterns and standards for cloud
integration and automation • Identify opportunities for
reuse and Improved efficiency• Engage with IT and Business
parters, product owners and stakeholders to create
meaningful roadmaps to ensure the most Important work Is
prioritized• Champion all Mastercards engineering
principles• Actively participate as a member of the Software
Engineering Guild sharing your knowledge, best practices,
ideas, and passion for technology• Help Identify and drive
meaningful behavior changing metrics All About You:• Years
of professional lead software engineering experience is
required with deep expertise in AWS and Azure, including
AI/GenAI services (SageMaker, Bedrock, AgentCore Azure ML,
Microsoft Foundry) and commercial/open source foundation
models.• Strong cloud architecture skills: services, access
controls, automation, integrations, and security in
regulated environments.• Solid understanding of MCP
protocol, and deployment of MCP servers at scale• Solid
understanding of AI Agents, and deployment of agents at
scale, managing context window size, security controls, A2A
protocol.• Extensive GenAI/LLM experience: model evaluation,
governance, tuning, API patterns, orchestration, agents,
RAG, prompt engineering, chunking, embeddings, vector
stores, model hubs, and fine tuned/open source models.•
Experience with AI/data platforms (Databricks, Iceberg, S3,
watsonx, or similar) and GenAI developer tools (GitHub
Copilot, Amazon Q, Claude).• Solid background in AI/ML, data
science, and analytics.• Strong SRE mindset with experience
building resilient, observable, automated cloud solutions.•
Proficient in web service design, containerization,
ephemeral architectures, and Kubernetes (native and
managed).• Proven ability to deliver solutions in complex
multi domain environments and clearly communicate technical
concepts.• Experience mentoring engineers through pairing,
code reviews, and design reviews.• Skilled in cloud native
modernization and migration from legacy systems.• Effective
in navigating large organizations; strong written and verbal
communication.• Deep understanding of Agile/Scrum,
Waterfall, and full SDLC processes.• Advocates for standard
software patterns, reuse of common libraries, open source
contribution, and strong performance engineering practices.•
Familiar with development productivity metrics and methods
to improve velocity, quality, and release frequency.•
Proficient in CDK, Bicep, and Terraform• Confident in public
speaking, presentation, and ability to articulate complex
topic to wide audience. #AI1 Mastercard is a merit-based,
inclusive, equal opportunity employer that considers
applicants without regard to gender, gender identity, sexual
orientation, race, ethnicity, disabled or veteran status, or
any other characteristic protected by law. We hire the most
qualified candidate for the role. In the US or Canada, if
you require accommodations or assistance to complete the
online application process or during the recruitment
process, please contact
reasonable_accommodation@mastercard.com and identify the
type of accommodation or assistance you are requesting. Do
not include any medical or health information in this email.
The Reasonable Accommodations team will respond to your
email promptly. Corporate Security Responsibility All
activities involving access to Mastercard assets,
information, and networks comes with an inherent risk to the
organization and, therefore, it is expected that every
person working for, or on behalf of, Mastercard is
responsible for information security and must:

- Abide by Mastercard's security policies and practices;
- Ensure the confidentiality and integrity of the information
  being accessed;
- Report any suspected information security violation or
  breach, and
- Complete all periodic mandatory security trainings in
  accordance with Mastercard's guidelines.

In line with Mastercard's total compensation philosophy and
assuming that the job will be performed in the US, the
successful candidate will be offered a competitive base
salary and may be eligible for an annual bonus or
commissions depending on the role. The base salary offered
may vary depending on multiple factors, including but not
limited to location, job-related knowledge, skills, and
experience. Mastercard benefits for full time (and certain
part time) employees generally include: insurance (including
medical, prescription drug, dental, vision, disability, life
insurance); flexible spending account and health savings
account; paid leaves (including 16 weeks of new parent leave
and up to 20 days of bereavement leave); 80 hours of Paid
Sick and Safe Time, 25 days of vacation time and 5 personal
days, pro-rated based on date of hire; 10 annual paid U.S.
observed holidays; 401k with a best-in-class company match;
deferred compensation for eligible roles; fitness
reimbursement or on-site fitness facilities; eligibility for
tuition reimbursement; and many more. Mastercard benefits
for interns generally include: 56 hours of Paid Sick and
Safe Time; jury duty leave; and on-site fitness facilities
in some locations. Pay Ranges New York City, New York:
$168,000 - $277,000 USD
responsibilities: Design, configure, and implement a Gen AI platform including MLOps/LLMOps for LLMs both commercial and open source
Ensure alignment to appropriate patterns and standards for cloud integration and automation
Identify opportunities for reuse and improved efficiency in AI platform development
Engage with IT and business partners, product owners and stakeholders to create meaningful roadmaps
Champion Mastercard engineering principles and participate in Software Engineering Guild sharing best practices
Help identify and drive meaningful behavior changing metrics for the AI platform
use_cases: Generative AI platform for Large Language Models serving enterprise-wide use cases
MCP server deployment at scale for protocol-based AI interactions
AI Agents deployment with context window management and A2A protocol support
RAG systems with prompt engineering, chunking, embeddings, and vector stores
Model evaluation, governance, tuning, and orchestration for commercial and open source models
ai_classification_reasoning:
This role is directly building a Generative AI/LLM platform
including MLOps/LLMOps infrastructure. The engineer will
implement model deployment, orchestration, RAG systems, and
agent deployment - working directly ON AI systems rather
than just supporting them.
